categories.summary: The discussion segment shows limited technical depth, with most exchanges being brief and non-technical. The main substantive contribution came from jgonly1_89829, who promoted Hatcher.host as a deployment platform for Eliza agents. According to jgonly1_89829, Hatcher.host is in free beta and provides a no-code, serverless solution for deploying Eliza agents or Milady agents without requiring users to manage servers, write code, or maintain hardware. The platform is positioned as a cloud computing solution that simplifies agent deployment and management while giving users control over their agents. cryptovalutchik_ inquired about the elizabao_ai project, seeking clarification on what the project is and what users can currently do with it, but received no direct technical answer. baogerbao noted increased interest in elizabao, mentioning that many people are asking about it. satsbased made brief comments about breakthroughs happening and the "eliza effect" becoming real, though no specifics were provided. The early portion of the chat contained vague complaints from ame05230523 about unspecified parties talking without taking action, but this lacked technical context. Overall, the segment demonstrates community interest in Eliza-related projects but contains minimal technical discussion beyond the Hatcher.host platform description.

faqs.question: what exactly is this project, and what can I as a user do there at this moment?

helpInteractions.context: Users seeking to understand how to deploy and use Eliza agents without technical complexity
helpInteractions.resolution: Introduced Hatcher.host as a free beta platform that eliminates the need for coding, server management, or hardware, providing ready-to-use cloud-based agent deployment

actionItems.description: Explore Hatcher.host platform for no-code Eliza agent deployment in free beta

categories.summary: This Discord chat segment shows minimal technical discussion and primarily consists of community questions about tokenomics and project updates. The main substantive exchange involved questions about ElizaOs token utility, airdrops, buybacks, and Jeju gas fees. Odilitime, appearing to be a team member, responded that some airdrops are planned but details aren't available yet, and there are no plans beyond what was previously laid out. Community members expressed frustration about lack of clarity on buyback timing and Jeju gas fee implementation. One user (huey79ng) encountered technical issues with wallet verification on Collab.land and sought support. The rest of the chat consisted of promotional messages, social media links, general greetings, and community sentiment posts encouraging holders to trust the project and buy during price dips. No concrete technical implementations, code discussions, or problem-solving occurred during this segment.

faqs.question: Holding ElizaOs for future airdrops?
When will details about the token utility be released?
Buyback — no one knows when it'll happen, Jeju gas fee — no one knows when it'll come out. Is that it?

actionItems.description: Provide details on planned airdrops
Release information about token utility details

Provide update on Jeju gas fee implementation

categories.summary: A Discord chat segment from the #💬-coders channel focused on ElizaOS v2 Socket.IO integration challenges. The main technical discussion centered on shah0406's detailed inquiry about the Socket.IO messaging protocol for ElizaOS v2. After extensive debugging, they discovered the correct messaging pattern: using socket.emit('message', {type: 1, payload: {...}}) for ROOM_JOINING and type: 2 for SEND_MESSAGE, rather than direct emits. Authentication requires {entityId: UUID} in socket options. Shah0406 is running a custom dashboard setup with ElizaOS v2 using @elizaos/plugin-openai, local Qwen3.5 via Nosana GPU on port 3001, and a dashboard on port 8080 with Socket.IO and HTTP polling fallback. They requested canonical documentation for the complete message type enum, required vs optional payload fields, and programmatic DM channel creation without the official UI. Odilitime suggested checking https://docs.elizaos.ai and using Cursor AI to query the codebase directly, noting they are currently focused on v3 development. Theredwizarddev shared an OrbisAPI Twitter link at the start of the conversation.

faqs.question: What is the canonical reference for ElizaOS v2 Socket.IO messaging protocol, including event names, payload schemas for type 1 (ROOM_JOINING) and type 2 (SEND_MESSAGE), and how to programmatically create a DM channel?
What is the complete message type enum for ElizaOS v2 Socket.IO (1=ROOM_JOINING, 2=SEND_MESSAGE, others)?
What are the required vs optional payload fields for each Socket.IO message type in ElizaOS v2?
What is the correct way to discover/create a DM channel programmatically in ElizaOS v2 without using the official UI?

helpInteractions.context: Shah0406 needed documentation for ElizaOS v2 Socket.IO protocol including message types, payload schemas, and programmatic DM channel creation
helpInteractions.resolution: Directed to https://docs.elizaos.ai and suggested using Cursor AI to query the codebase directly for answers

actionItems.description: Document complete ElizaOS v2 Socket.IO message type enum (1=ROOM_JOINING, 2=SEND_MESSAGE, and others)
Document required vs optional payload fields for each Socket.IO message type in ElizaOS v2
Document programmatic DM channel creation/discovery in ElizaOS v2 without using official UI
Add Socket.IO authentication requirements ({entityId: UUID} in socket options) to official docs

categories.summary: The discussion centered on concerns about ElizaOS project status and a Bitget exchange delisting announcement. Community members questioned why the ElizaOS X account hadn't posted since March, with some expressing bearish sentiment. The main issue was Bitget's announcement to delist ELIZAOS/USDT trading pair on April 17, 2026, along with 6 other pairs. Odilitime clarified that the team never communicated with Bitget about the listing. He addressed misconceptions about the project being a "low mcap gem" or "unknown token," stating they aimed for blue chip status. Odilitime acknowledged communication challenges, noting the team continues working as they did last year but struggles to convey their vision to the market. He expressed concern that investors don't understand or believe in what they're building, attributing lack of buy pressure to this disconnect. While taking responsibility for communication failures, he suggested the project might be a "failed experiment" if they can't bridge the understanding gap with the market.

faqs.question: Why hasn't elizaOS X posted anything since March?
Why is Bitget delisting elizaos/usdt?
Is there any delisting news on Bitget regarding $ELIZAOS?

helpInteractions.context: Bitget delisting announcement details
helpInteractions.resolution: Provided official delisting information: Bitget delisting 7 trading pairs including ELIZAOS/USDT on April 17, 2026, 10:00 AM UTC

helpInteractions.context: Clarifying relationship with Bitget exchange
helpInteractions.resolution: Confirmed the team never communicated with Bitget about the listing

helpInteractions.context: Finding delisting information
helpInteractions.resolution: Advised to search "bitget elizaos/usdt delisting" for details

actionItems.description: Improve communication strategy to help market understand project vision and goals
Increase posting frequency on X/Twitter social media

categories.summary: The channel had minimal technical activity. The main substantive contribution was from shieldai, who introduced AgentAudit, a blockchain-based audit logging solution for AI agents designed for production ElizaOS deployments. The system provides immutable, tamper-proof logging of agent actions with EU AI Act compliance. It's currently deployed on Arbitrum Sepolia and Mantle Sepolia testnets at address 0x25ac2ab1369001F9C847e65f010B6e4f4340d78a. The TypeScript SDK is available, with a simple API that logs agent actions directly to the blockchain with metadata. An ElizaOS plugin integration is planned for release within the week. The implementation targets autonomous agents handling real transactions where auditability is critical. At the end of the segment, 0xnemian requested assistance with running a custom plugin but received no response during this timeframe. The rest of the chat consisted of brief exchanges about a scam message.

faqs.question: How do I run a custom plugin?

actionItems.description: Complete and release ElizaOS plugin for AgentAudit integration
Assist with running custom plugin implementation

categories.summary: The discussion centered on tokenomics clarity and communication issues affecting the ElizaOS project. joshisgood77 questioned the connection between the product and token, which odilitime acknowledged exists but admitted people don't like how it's connected. The conversation revealed a BitGet delisting situation, with ellince8 explaining it was due to low trading volume (10-50k per day) rather than scam concerns, as market makers need sufficient trading fees. odilitime took responsibility for communication failures and announced plans for a call with Shaw next week to restructure Discord and X account management. rainman1001 suggested allowing trusted community members to handle social media admin, citing success on another project. chulylooly emphasized the critical need for clear tokenomics messaging and connecting dots for investors, noting vague tokenomics kill projects quickly. The team discussed merchandise, with baogerbao creating elizaOK merch using elizacloud. huzaiii_founder presented a quantum-safe authentication gateway for AI agents on Solana using ML-KEM-768 (NIST FIPS 203), with live demo and SDK available. The migration period from ai16z to elizaos was confirmed as ended by odilitime.

faqs.question: What you're building and the token aren't connected at all. Why would investors buy it?
Why was there a delisting?
Is there any elizaOS merchandise available?
Can I still migrate my ai16z to elizaos?

helpInteractions.context: Confusion about delisting reasons and FUD spreading
helpInteractions.resolution: Clarified that delisting was due to low trading volume (10-50k per day) and market maker economics, not scam concerns

helpInteractions.context: Need for better social media and community communication
helpInteractions.resolution: Suggested allowing trusted community member to take over SM admin role, citing successful implementation on another project

helpInteractions.context: Looking for elizaOS merchandise
helpInteractions.resolution: Informed that no official merch exists but Bao made elizaOK merch

helpInteractions.context: Tokenomics clarity and investor communication issues
helpInteractions.resolution: Advised to simplify connection between product and token, clean up messaging, and show progress to investors

Clarify and simplify tokenomics connection between product and token
Implement quantum-safe authentication gateway for AI agents on Solana using ML-KEM-768

Improve communication and messaging to connect dots for investors

categories.summary: The channel discussion focused on local model implementation challenges and quantum-safe authentication integration. 0xnemian encountered persistent JSON parsing errors with Ollama text generation, showing repeated failures in XML parsing and missing required fields (thought/actions) across multiple retry attempts. The error manifested as 'Invalid JSON response' with the system unable to extract structured data from LLM responses. odilitime provided recommendations for local model parameters, suggesting Gemma, gptoss, and Qwen as working alternatives, and mentioned ongoing development of improved technology for local model support. huzaiii_founder announced a production-ready PQC (Post-Quantum Cryptography) authentication layer for ElizaOS agents via the bridgebase-sdk, offering quantum-safe identity with minimal integration code. The solution is deployed and accessible via Railway. odilitime inquired about branch specifics, likely regarding the error issues reported.

faqs.question: is there a minimum parameter recommended for local models?
Is this on the develop branch?

helpInteractions.context: 0xnemian experiencing Invalid JSON response errors with Ollama text generation and XML parsing failures
helpInteractions.resolution: Recommended using Gemma, gptoss, or Qwen models as working alternatives and mentioned developing better tech for local models

actionItems.description: Investigate and fix Invalid JSON response error in generateOllamaText causing XML parsing failures and missing thought/actions fields
Develop improved technology to help with local model support
Integrate PQC authentication layer (bridgebase-sdk) for quantum-safe identity in ElizaOS agents

categories.summary: The discussion centered around CROO Network's V1 Pioneers Program announcement by minorc, which provides a payment and marketplace infrastructure layer for ElizaOS agents. The technical stack includes a CLI-based SDK that auto-generates ERC-4337 compliant Account Abstraction wallets, agent registration capabilities, service listing functionality, and automated on-chain payment settlement. This addresses gaps in ElizaOS's native capabilities around payment processing, reputation systems, and agent discovery. The program offers USDT rewards ($10 base plus up to $50 for category winners) for the first 100 developers who test the SDK. The infrastructure is positioned as complementary to ElizaOS v2's multi-chain wallet and event-driven architecture. Additional topics included agent tokenization on Solana via Metaplex, questions about legacy token utility, and an offer from loko9567391 for assistance with agent workflows and LLM infrastructure in production environments.

faqs.question: Can I get a link for more info about the CROO Network V1 Pioneers Program?
What can I do with the old tokens now?

helpInteractions.context: Need for payment, reputation, and discovery infrastructure for ElizaOS agents
helpInteractions.resolution: Announced CROO Network V1 Pioneers Program with CLI SDK, ERC-4337 AA wallet generation, agent registration, service marketplace, and automated on-chain payments

helpInteractions.context: Issues with agent workflows and LLM infrastructure in production
helpInteractions.resolution: Offered direct assistance for troubleshooting production issues

helpInteractions.context: Question about utility of old tokens
helpInteractions.resolution: Confirmed no current utility for old tokens

actionItems.description: Test CROO Network SDK for agent commerce infrastructure integration with ElizaOS
Implement ERC-4337 Account Abstraction wallet integration for agent payments
Build agent marketplace with automated on-chain payment settlement
Provide link and documentation for CROO Network V1 Pioneers Program
Explore agent tokenization on Solana using Metaplex

categories.summary: A significant organizational transition was revealed when flykite shared leaked messages showing Shaw announced the shutdown of Eliza Labs on 4/12, citing market conditions, depleted treasury, and inability to reach revenue. However, odilitime clarified this represents a shift from paid development to community-driven open source work rather than complete shutdown. The core framework development continues with Shaw and odilitime leading efforts. Key technical work includes: ElizaOS v3 nearing completion, Spartan agent being developed for marketing automation with local model support for cost reduction, and plans for agentic business demos. The discussion revealed internal frustrations about previous team productivity issues and focus on UI over core agent functionality. Community members expressed continued support despite uncertainty. Technical discussion included x402 agent payment infrastructure on Solana, with huhuhu0621_67650 building a trust scoring service for agent wallets and deciding between USDC-SPL on Solana versus Base USDC for settlements. Stan0473 recommended supporting multiple chains to reduce friction. Odilitime mentioned repurposing Spartan technology built for previous projects, implementing cost reduction through local models, and potential revival of agentic DAO stack given improved AI models. The transition separates development discussion (Cozy devs) from economic/community discussion (main Discord). Security concerns were raised about scammers impersonating odilitime.

faqs.question: Is shaw around
Was building on ElizaOS but heard the team is fired and all work is stopping. Is that true?
Could we turn it to a dao?
is elizaOS shutting down?
if you're calling a paid endpoint via x402, do you prefer paying in USDC-SPL on Solana or are you fine holding Base USDC for settlements?

helpInteractions.context: Concerns about ElizaOS team being fired and development stopping
helpInteractions.resolution: Clarified that contributions are rising, git issues cleaned up, 1.x stable, 2.x in development, and transition is from paid to community-driven development

helpInteractions.context: Confusion about leaked shutdown messages
helpInteractions.resolution: Directed to check team working space to verify ongoing activity

helpInteractions.context: Understanding the 4/12 internal message about Labs shutdown
helpInteractions.resolution: Confirmed message is legitimate but explained it represents operational shift to stop paying some devs while community development continues

helpInteractions.context: Deciding payment chain support for x402 agent endpoint
helpInteractions.resolution: Recommended supporting both USDC-SPL and Base USDC, letting client decide chain to lower friction

helpInteractions.context: Confusion about whether ElizaOS is shutting down
helpInteractions.resolution: Clarified that project is expanding with separation of dev and economic discussions, devs now have space to polish work

categories.summary: The discussion centers on a pull request (#17) for the elizaos-plugins/plugin-anthropic repository. stan0473 submitted a PR adding two key features: a 'claude -p' command solution and missing reasoning type support. The PR introduces REASONING_SMALL_MODEL_TYPE (later renamed to TEXT_REASONING_SMALL_MODEL_TYPE) with fallback to the main model. odilitime reviewed the code, raised concerns about potential Anthropic account bans (which stan0473 confirmed was addressed), and identified two code issues via PR comments. After fixes, odilitime merged the PR and attempted to publish the package. The CI/CD pipeline encountered multiple build errors: first, a workspace dependency '@elizaos/core' not found error (attributed to alpha path changes), then a 'jsonrepair' resolution failure during browser build. odilitime worked on fixing package.json to resolve these issues. stan0473 discovered additional missing functionality and prepared another PR, also adding streaming support for the 'claude -p' command which handles streaming-json.

faqs.question: Will this get people's anthropic accounts banned?
Does REASONING_SMALL_MODEL_TYPE only apply to -p mode or both modes?
Is REASONING_SMALL_MODEL_TYPE like run custom prompt?

helpInteractions.context: Code review of PR #17 for plugin-anthropic
helpInteractions.resolution: odilitime caught 2 issues in the code and left comments on the PR for stan0473 to address

helpInteractions.context: Explaining the REASONING_SMALL_MODEL_TYPE specification and its fallback behavior
helpInteractions.resolution: Clarified that TYPE is a plugin specification and the model falls back to main model

helpInteractions.context: CI build failure with workspace dependency error
helpInteractions.resolution: Identified that alpha changed paths causing the '@elizaos/core' dependency issue

actionItems.description: Fix REASONING_SMALL_MODEL_TYPE naming to TEXT_REASONING_SMALL_MODEL_TYPE (same for LARGE)
Fix package.json to resolve workspace dependency '@elizaos/core' not found error
Resolve 'jsonrepair' dependency issue in browser build
Add streaming support for claude -p command with streaming-json handling
Create new PR to address missed important functionality
Publish plugin-anthropic package after build fixes

categories.summary: The Discord discussion channel had minimal technical activity during this period. The most notable technical contribution came from huhuhu0621_67650, who announced the release of an Express middleware package called TrustGate for ERC-8004 trust scoring for x402 agents. The package is available on npm as @oceanrun/trustgate and can be integrated with a single line of code to add trust gating to Express endpoints. The community dealt with security concerns, with waiser0165 and jvik identifying scam attempts in the AIRDROP channel, which odilitime confirmed as fraudulent. Stan0473 indicated active moderation efforts to remove scam content. Mladcepes raised a question about wallet address and GitHub profile linking issues, though this received no direct response. Ricky_05784 mentioned learning experiences with OpenClaw and API costs versus OAUTH implementation, seeking work opportunities. The channel showed light activity with general greetings and acknowledgments throughout the day.
